# NCERT Summary: Grammar: Tenses and Voice

## Chapter at a Glance
This chapter introduces the fundamental concepts of Tenses and Voice in English grammar, enabling students to understand and use them correctly. The chapter focuses on the three main tenses - Present, Past, and Future - and their various forms, including the Simple, Continuous, Perfect, and Perfect Continuous forms. Additionally, it covers the Passive Voice, describing how to form it and its different forms. The chapter helps students develop their ability to use tenses and voice correctly in sentences and write coherent paragraphs. By the end of this chapter, students should be able to identify and use the correct tense and voice in various contexts.

## Section-wise Summary
### Present Tense
- **Present Simple**: Used to express general truths, routines, and habits. It is used to talk about permanent or general situations. (e.g., He eats breakfast at 7 a.m. every day.)
- **Present Continuous**: Used to express actions that are happening at the moment of speaking or actions that are temporary. (e.g., She is studying for her exams.)
- **Present Perfect**: Used to express actions that started in the past and continue up to the present moment. (e.g., I have been studying English since last year.)
- **Present Perfect Continuous**: Used to express actions that started in the past and continue up to the present moment. (e.g., She has been working on this project since last week.)

### Past Tense
- **Past Simple**: Used to express completed actions in the past. (e.g., I went to the park yesterday.)
- **Past Continuous**: Used to express actions that were happening at a particular time in the past. (e.g., I was watching TV when the doorbell rang.)
- **Past Perfect**: Used to express actions that happened before another action in the past. (e.g., I had eaten before I went to bed.)
- **Past Perfect Continuous**: Used to express actions that started before another action in the past and continued up to that time. (e.g., I had been studying for three hours before I took a break.)

### Future Tense
- **Future Simple**: Used to express actions that will happen in the future. (e.g., I will go to the store tomorrow.)
- **Future Continuous**: Used to express actions that will be happening at a particular time in the future. (e.g., I will be studying English next year.)
- **Future Perfect**: Used to express actions that will be completed before another action in the future. (e.g., I will have finished my project by next week.)
- **Future Perfect Continuous**: Used to express actions that will have started before another action in the future and continue up to that time. (e.g., I will have been studying for two years by the time I graduate.)

### Voice
- **Active Voice**: Subject performs the action. (e.g., I eat breakfast every morning.)
- **Passive Voice**: Subject receives the action. (e.g., Breakfast is eaten by me every morning.)

### Forms of Passive Voice
- **Simple Passive**: Used to express completed actions in the past. (e.g., The book was read by her last night.)
- **Continuous Passive**: Used to express actions that were happening at a particular time in the past. (e.g., The book was being read by her when I arrived.)
- **Perfect Passive**: Used to express actions that happened before another action in the past. (e.g., The book had been read by her before I arrived.)
- **Perfect Continuous Passive**: Used to express actions that started before another action in the past and continued up to that time. (e.g., The book had been being read by her for three hours before I arrived.)

## Important NCERT Exercises Insight
The NCERT exercises cover various topics, including:
- Identifying and forming different tenses (Simple, Continuous, Perfect, and Perfect Continuous) for Present, Past, and Future.
- Identifying and forming different forms of the Passive Voice (Simple, Continuous, Perfect, and Perfect Continuous).
- Using the correct tense and voice in sentences.
- Writing coherent paragraphs using the correct tense and voice.

Key concepts to remember:
- Use the correct tense and voice to convey the intended meaning.
- Identify and form different tenses and forms of the Passive Voice correctly.
- Practice writing coherent paragraphs using the correct tense and voice.

## NCERT Exemplar Focus
The NCERT Exemplar covers additional topics, including:
- Using the correct tense and voice in complex sentences.
- Identifying and forming the Passive Voice in complex sentences.
- Writing descriptive paragraphs using the correct tense and voice.

## Board Exam Relevance
The Board Exam typically carries marks from 10-15 for Grammar questions, including Tenses and Voice. Frequently asked topics include:
- Identifying and forming different tenses (Simple, Continuous, Perfect, and Perfect Continuous) for Present, Past, and Future.
- Identifying and forming different forms of the Passive Voice (Simple, Continuous, Perfect, and Perfect Continuous).
- Using the correct tense and voice in sentences.
- Writing coherent paragraphs using the correct tense and voice.
